testcase: Assistance with Lint Diff for "ParametersInPost" Rule
query:
title: Assistance with Lint Diff for "ParametersInPost" Rule

question: Hi Team,
II am encountering a Lint Diff error in my PR [Swagger changes for Express Route Link Failover by shubhika1795 · Pull Request #26403 · Azure/azure…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/pull/26403)
Previously, I made similar changes for other POST APIs in the [Swagger changes for expressroute site failover simulation by shubytia · Pull Request #30731 · Azure…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/30731/files#diff-8243308eb8c1b41acb8021a4e3ee2baaa9ff92873e7daef3820ac7e2756d2691) and those did not trigger any lint errors. However, with this recent PR, I am seeing failures.
Could someone please advise or help me understand how to resolve this?
Thank you
ground_truth:
Based on your description, you are encountering a LintDiff error in your PR for Express Route Link Failover, while similar POST API changes for expressroute site failover simulation did not trigger lint errors. The difference in LintDiff results can be due to several factors:
LintDiff Rule Applicability and Spec Differences: LintDiff checks are sensitive to the exact OpenAPI structure, operation details, and how the API is modeled. Even if two APIs are both POST and seem similar, small differences in request/response schemas, operation paths, or long-running operation (LRO) patterns can trigger different rules. For example, rules like PatchBodyParameterSchema, AvoidAnonymousTypes, or EvenSegmentedPathForPutOperation may only trigger if certain patterns are present in the new API.
TypeSpec/OpenAPI Generation Issues: If your spec is generated from TypeSpec, there are known issues where the OpenAPI emitter or LintDiff rules may produce false positives or errors for certain patterns (see the knowledge context for details). For example, using Record<unknown> or anonymous types can trigger LintDiff errors that may not appear in other APIs if their schemas are more explicit.
Suppression and Resolution Guidance:
For management plane (ARM) specs, you should generally fix LintDiff errors rather than suppress them, unless the error is a known false positive or falls under an exception (such as anonymous types in 202 responses from LROs).
For data plane specs, suppressions are allowed for certain rules (see knowledge context).
To resolve the error, review the raw LintDiff log for your PR and identify the exact rule and JSON path causing the failure. Then, compare the failing API's schema and operation details with the previous successful API. Look for differences in required properties, response types, or path structure.
If the error is a known false positive (see the knowledge context for rules like PatchBodyParametersSchema, EvenSegmentedPathForPutOperation, etc.), you may suppress it with a clear reason. Otherwise, update your Swagger/TypeSpec to comply with the rule.
Next Steps:
Check the exact LintDiff error code and message in your PR's CI logs.
Use the guidance in the knowledge context to determine if the error is a known issue or requires a spec fix.
If you need to suppress, follow the scoped suppression instructions (convert the error's JSON path to a suppression entry).
If you need further help, provide the exact error code and message for more targeted advice.
If you share the specific LintDiff error code and the relevant OpenAPI snippet, I can provide more detailed resolution steps.
References
[Adding Scoped Suppressions | Azure Rest Api Specs Wiki](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/wiki/Swagger-LintDiff)
[API-Spec-Review-FAQ.md | Azure Rest Api Specs Wiki](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/wiki/API-Spec-Review-FAQ)
[Suppress validation failures on a PR | Azure-sdk-docs-eng](https://eng.ms/docs/products/azure-developer-experience/design/specs-pr-guides/pr-suppressions)

expected_references.title: Adding Scoped Suppressions | Azure Rest Api Specs Wiki
API-Spec-Review-FAQ.md | Azure Rest Api Specs Wiki
Suppress validation failures on a PR | Azure-sdk-docs-eng

testcase: .NET build failing
query:
title: .NET build failing

question: Hi 
General
I’m unable to resolve the .NET build failure in the PRs mentioned below. I’ve tried multiple approaches, but the error persists and the error code is unclear.
Please can someone guide me 
[Adding swagger changes for Express Route Link Failover by shubhika1795 · Pull Request #39840 · Azur…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/39840)
[Swagger changes for Vwan Express Route Gateway Failover and Insights by shubhika1795 · Pull Request…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/39841)
ground_truth:
Based on your description and the provided PRs, the .NET build failure is likely due to unresolved validation or lintdiff errors in your Swagger/OpenAPI specification. Here’s how you should proceed:
1. Check the Pipeline Logs for Error Details:
Go to the Azure Pipelines build/checks for your PR and click on the failing .NET SDK validation step. Use the 'View details' link to see the exact error messages. The logs will usually indicate the specific schema or naming violation.
2. Fix LintDiff and Validation Errors:
If the error is related to lintdiff, you need to address the specific issues flagged by the linter. These are often related to breaking changes, missing documentation, or schema mismatches. The error message will point to the exact location in your spec.
For naming violations or schema issues, refer to the [Azure SDK Naming Conventions](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/doc/dev/Mgmt-Naming-Conventions.md) and [Polishing Configurations](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/doc/dev/Mgmt-Polishing-Configurations.md) to ensure your definitions match the expected patterns.
3. Suppressions (if absolutely necessary):
If you believe the error is a false positive or cannot be fixed immediately, you can add a suppression. However, the suppression path must match the exact element the error is pointing to. See the guidance on [Suppress validation failures on a PR](https://eng.ms/docs/products/azure-developer-experience/design/specs-pr-guides/pr-suppressions).
4. Get Help from Language Teams:
If the error code is unclear or you cannot resolve the issue, reach out to the .NET language team via their [Teams channel](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/channel/19%3a7b87fb348f224b37b6206fa9d89a105b%40thread.skype/Language%2520-%2520DotNet?groupId=3e17dcb0-4257-4a30-b843-77f47f1d4121&tenantId=72f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47) and provide the PR link and error details.

Summary of Next Steps:
- Review the .NET build logs for specific error messages.
- Fix any schema, naming, or lintdiff issues as indicated.
- If needed, add precise suppressions for validation errors.
- Contact the .NET SDK team if you are blocked.

This process should help you identify and resolve the .NET build failures in your PRs.

expected_references.title: Suppress validation failures on a PR

testcase: unexpected results after running prettier
query:
title: unexpected results after running prettier

question: I'm updating a sample in the Azure Maps [Search - Get Geocoding Batch - REST API  ](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/maps/search/get-geocoding-batch?view=rest-maps-2025-01-01&tabs=HTTP)
 
The [PR](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/40044) validation flagged an error : Code style issues found, please run prettier.
```
{
  code: 'OBJECT_ADDITIONAL_PROPERTIES',
  message: 'Additional properties not allowed: addressline',
  schemaUrl: 'specification/maps/data-plane/Search/stable/2025-01-01/search.json',
  exampleUrl: 'specification/maps/data-plane/Search/stable/2025-01-01/examples/ForwardGeocodingBatch.json',
  schemaPosition: { line: 685, column: 34 },
  schemaJsonPath: '#/additionalProperties',
  examplePosition: { line: 5, column: 34 },
  exampleJsonPath: '$parameters.geocodingBatchRequestBody',
  severity: 0,
  source: 'request',
  operationId: 'Search_GetGeocodingBatch',
  level: '\
```
So I ran it... I wasn't sure what changes it made, so I ran a "git diff" in git bash, and don't understand the response:
 
This went on and on....
 
I'm not sure what this means, or how it relates to my PR or the file that should have been updated by prettier... Any ideas?
ground_truth:
The error 'Code style issues found, please run prettier' means that the automated validation detected formatting issues in your files (such as indentation, spacing, or line breaks) that do not comply with the project's code style rules. The tool 'prettier' is used to automatically fix these formatting issues.
When you run 'prettier', it reformats the files in place. To see what changed, you can use 'git diff', which shows the differences between your current working directory and the last committed state. The output you pasted (the long diff) is showing exactly what lines were changed by prettier in your files. For example, lines starting with '-' were removed or changed, and lines starting with '+' were added or changed. The file path at the top (e.g., 'specification/maps/data-plane/Search/stable/2025-01-01/examples/ForwardGeocodingBatch.json') tells you which file was modified.
What you should do:
Review the changes shown by 'git diff' to confirm they are only formatting changes (no unintended content changes).
If everything looks good, stage the changes with 'git add ' for each modified file.
Commit the changes with 'git commit -m "Apply prettier formatting"'.
Push your commit to update your PR.
This will resolve the 'prettier' check failure in your PR validation. The formatting changes are expected and required for the PR to pass validation.

testcase: Unable to load data plane API template
query:
title: Unable to load data plane API template

question: Team, When I click the Data Plane API, it doesn't load the template. This is the link to the PR  https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/40202?expand=1&template=data_plane_template.md
 
I followed the contributing guide, not sure if there is anything I may have missed. Appreciate your help.
ground_truth:
Based on the PR content and your description, the issue is that the Data Plane API PR template did not load when you created your PR, even though you followed the contributing guide. This is a common issue and is usually caused by one of the following:
GitHub PR Template Loading Behavior: GitHub only loads the PR template automatically if you create the PR using the "New Pull Request" button and do not pre-fill the PR description. If you edit the PR description after creation, or if you use the API or certain tools, the template may not appear.
Template Location and Naming: The PR templates for azure-rest-api-specs are located in .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/. You must select either the "Control Plane (ARM) API" or "Data Plane API" template when creating the PR. If you do not select a template, GitHub will use the default or none.
Branch Targeting: If your PR targets a branch other than main, or if you create the PR from a fork, sometimes GitHub does not show the template selector.
Resolution Steps:
When creating your PR, ensure you use the GitHub web UI and select the appropriate template from the dropdown ("Data Plane API").
If the template does not load, you can manually copy the template content from .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/data_plane_template.md and paste it into your PR description.
Double-check that your PR is targeting the correct branch (main for public repo, or the appropriate branch for private repo).
This does not affect the technical review of your PR, but using the template helps reviewers understand your submission and ensures you provide all required information.
If you continue to have issues, you can proceed without the template, but reviewers may ask you to provide the information manually.

testcase: SDK Generation Tracking
query:
title: SDK Generation Tracking

question: Regenerated SDK in attempt to retry failing .NET SDK generation. In the process this retriggered the generation for all other language, even though those Generation PRs had already been completed and merged, and the SDKs had been released. 
 
I have closed these new regenerated PRs and linked the previously merged PRs, however in the Release Planner "Generate SDKs" tab, these are still pulling the status of the new PRs rather than the original one, is there anyway to re-establish the link to the original PRs?
ground_truth:
Based on the provided knowledge, there is currently no documented mechanism within the Release Planner to manually re-link or re-establish the association to the original, previously merged SDK generation PRs after new, redundant PRs have been created and closed. The Release Planner tracks the status of the most recent PRs generated by its automation, and once new PRs are triggered (for example, by retrying SDK generation), it updates its tracking to those new PRs. Closing the new PRs and linking the old ones in comments is desirable for context, but the Release Planner UI will continue to reference the latest PRs it created.
If you encounter this scenario, the recommended approach is to add a comment in the affected PRs referencing the previously merged PRs (as you have done), which is considered desirable but not mandatory. There is planned work to reduce occurrences of this scenario, but as of now, manual re-linking in the Release Planner is not supported.
If this is blocking your release or causing confusion, you can reach out to the Azure SDK Onboarding channel for support or report a bug directly in Release Planner. For now, proceed with your release using the previously merged PRs and document the linkage in comments for audit and review purposes.

testcase: Question: Reporting TypeSpec Java Generator Bug - Ambiguous Method Calls in Generated Tests
query:
title: Question: Reporting TypeSpec Java Generator Bug - Ambiguous Method Calls in Generated Tests

question: I'm working on the KeyVault Certificates TypeSpec specification and encountered a bug in the auto-generated Java test code. Before filing a formal issue, I wanted to reach out to understand the proper reporting process and whether this is a known issue.
Issue Summary:
When generating Java SDK code for KeyVault Certificates (API version 2025-06-01-preview), the TypeSpec Java generator creates test files with compilation errors due to ambiguous method calls with null parameters.
Changes: KeyVault Certificates: Add URIs and IP addresses to SubjectAlternativeNames by rohitsinghal · Pul…
Example:
// Generated test code:
PagedIterable<CertificateIssuerItem> response = keyVaultClient.getCertificateIssuers(null);
 
// Compilation error:
reference to getCertificateIssuers is ambiguous
both method getCertificateIssuers(RequestOptions) in KeyVaultClient 
and method getCertificateIssuers(Integer) in KeyVaultClient matchest Files:
GetCertificateIssuersTests.java (line 19)
GetCertificateVersionsTests.java (line 20)
Questions:
Is this a known issue in the TypeSpec Java generator?
What is the proper channel to report generator bugs? (GitHub repo/issue tracker?)
Should generated test files with @Disabled annotations be skipped during compilation, or should the generator be fixed?
Is there a workaround for this in the meantime?
Technical Details:
TypeSpec compiler: v1.9.0
@azure-tools/typespec-java: latest
Java: JDK 21
Repository: azure-rest-api-specs
Specification: Security.KeyVault.Certificates
Thank you for your guidance!
ground_truth:
This ambiguous method call issue in auto-generated Java test code (where null is passed and multiple overloads match) is not documented as a known bug in the TypeSpec Java generator in the current knowledge context. The proper channel to report generator bugs is to file an issue in the relevant GitHub repository—typically the TypeSpec Java emitter repo or the Azure SDK for Java repo, depending on where the bug originates. For TypeSpec generator bugs, use the TypeSpec Discussion Teams channel or the GitHub issue tracker for the TypeSpec Java emitter.
Generated test files with @Disabled annotations are not meant to be skipped during compilation; they are skipped during test execution, but compilation errors must still be fixed. The generator should be fixed to avoid ambiguous method calls, either by generating explicit overloads or by using non-null parameters that match only one overload.
As a workaround, you can manually edit the generated test files to specify the intended parameter type (e.g., use null cast to RequestOptions or Integer, or provide a valid value), but this is not a permanent solution. The best approach is to report the bug so the generator can be updated to handle ambiguous overloads correctly.
Summary of recommended actions:
Report the bug in the TypeSpec Java emitter repo or Teams channel.
Do not rely on @Disabled to skip compilation errors; fix the generator.
Workaround: manually edit test files to resolve ambiguity, but push for a generator fix.

testcase: Request for Priority ARM API review for az sql 20250201-preview release
query:
title: Request for Priority ARM API review for az sql 20250201-preview release

question: Can you please review this spec PR for 20250201-preview version release PR for Az SQL ARM API?
It is critical for SQLConn and has been blocked for a long time:
https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/38646
ground_truth:
Based on the provided PR content for the Azure SQL ARM API 20250201-preview release, here is a review summary:
Breaking Change Status:
The PR is labeled with BreakingChangeReviewRequired and BreakingChange-Approved-Previously. According to the workflow, breaking changes must be reviewed and approved before proceeding. The comment from 'mikekistler' confirms that breaking changes were previously reviewed and approved in PR #35794. This satisfies the breaking change requirement.
ARM Review Status:
The PR has both ARMReview and WaitForARMFeedback labels, indicating it is in the ARM review queue. The review from 'jeremyfrosti' is marked as APPROVED, which is a positive signal.
TypeSpec Conversion Notice:
There is a reminder that all active Azure services must convert to TypeSpec by March 31, 2026. This does not block the current PR but is important for future planning.
Next Steps:
Since breaking changes are approved and the PR is in the ARM review queue, the next step is for the ARM reviewer (on-call) to complete their review and add the ARMSignedOff label. If the PR remains blocked, you may ping the ARM reviewer or use ARM API Review Office Hours (https://aka.ms/armofficehoursinfo) for escalation.
General Guidance:
Ensure all CI checks are passing before requesting a re-review.
If you are stuck, follow the instructions in the "Next Steps to Merge" comment or see https://aka.ms/azsdk/pr-getting-help.
Summary: Your PR is in the correct state for ARM review, with breaking changes already approved. The ARM reviewer needs to finalize the review. If urgent, escalate via office hours or Teams channel as per process.

ground_truth:

testcase: azp run prepare-pipelines didn't generate a pipeline for python?
query:
title: azp run prepare-pipelines didn't generate a pipeline for python?

question: Hello,
 
Here is the python PR that was merged and for which pipeline generation command was run before merging. However I can't find a pipeline created for it. Is the release process different for python. Copilot suggested to use python - partner-release pipeline but couldn't find it mentioned in any other wiki.
 
[[AutoPR azure-mgmt-monitorslis]-generated-from-SDK Generation - Python-6195144 by azure-sdk · Pull …](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/pull/46478)
ground_truth:
For Python management-plane SDKs (`azure-mgmt-*`), use the standard service-directory unified pipeline, not `python-partner-release` (which is only for packages released outside the mono-repo). With `sdk/monitor/ci.yml` listing `azure-mgmt-monitorslis` under `Artifacts`, the release pipeline is `python - monitor` in the `azure-sdk` org → `internal` project in Azure DevOps; the same pipeline releases all monitor packages.

To release: open the `python - monitor` pipeline, click **Run pipeline** targeting the commit on `main` containing the merged PR, select only the `azure-mgmt-monitorslis` artifact if prompted, let build/signing complete, then approve the release stage to publish to PyPI. If the pipeline doesn't exist, open a no-op PR touching `sdk/monitor/ci.yml` and re-run `/azp run prepare-pipelines` to (re)generate it.

---

testcase: Some questions related to creating specs for new RP
query:
title: Some questions related to creating specs for new RP

question: I'm creating a new RP and working on its specs, here is my draft PR and the questions below
[API Specification for new RP Microsoft.MAISearch by nader-m-elhadedy · Pull Request #28434 · Azure/…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/pull/28434)
 
1- I understood that I only need to push the typespec files, and the swagger specs will be auto-generated, is that right? and will it be generated after the PR is merged? as I didn't see them in the draft
2- Is it a must for RP name to start with "Microsoft.*" or not?
3- Do I need to have all checks passing in the draft PR so I make it ready for review then it enters the queue for review?
4- Do I need to do any follow-up after it's being ready for review?
ground_truth:
For new services, TypeSpec is the source of truth, but both the TypeSpec source and the generated OpenAPI must be checked into the specs repo in your PR — do not rely on OpenAPI being generated only after merge. For ARM management-plane RPs, the namespace must follow the `Microsoft.<ServiceName>` pattern, so `Microsoft.MAISearch` is fine. You do not need every check green before requesting review, but you should fix linting/ARM modeling failures first since reviewers will require them anyway. After marking the PR ready for review, address reviewer feedback, push updates, and explicitly request re-review via GitHub; no additional special step is needed to stay in the review queue. The repo sample at [specification/widget/resource-manager/Microsoft.Widget/Widget](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/tree/main/specification/widget/resource-manager/Microsoft.Widget/Widget) is a helpful reference.

---

testcase: How to handle pre-existing LintDiff errors surfaced by v1→v2 file move?
query:
title: How to handle pre-existing LintDiff errors surfaced by v1→v2 file move?

question: I'm working on migrating the HybridAKS TypeSpec project from v1 to v2 folder structure. The restructuring itself is complete and TypeSpec compiles successfully, but I'm hitting LintDiff CI failures.
 
The issue: LintDiff is diff-based and treats file moves as delete + add. So all pre-existing lint violations in the older hand-written swagger files (2023-11-15-preview and 2024-01-01 stable) are now flagged as if they're newly introduced errors. These violations have always existed on main and were previously accepted.
 
The 10 errors flagged are: PathForTrackedResourceTypes, TrackedExtensionResourcesAreNotAllowed, PatchResponseCodes, XmsPageableForListCalls, AvoidAdditionalProperties, and LroLocationHeader.
 
What's the recommended approach here? Should I suppress these errors?
 
Here is my PR: [Migrate Arm API specs to v2 file structure by connievi · Pull Request #42990 · Azure/azure-rest-api…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/42990)
 
Please let me know if I should post this question somewhere else!
 
cc Pradip Dhara
ground_truth:
Going forward, permanent source-based LintDiff suppressions are required rather than relying on the diff feature of LintDiff, which doesn't scale beyond a single PR. Prefer actually fixing the real guideline violations if the service team can safely do so. Otherwise, add permanent scoped LintDiff suppressions in the AutoRest configuration using the new suppression syntax with a `where` clause targeting each specific JSON path reported by LintDiff. The suppression reason should explain **why** the suppression is needed (e.g. "API was designed before guideline existed" or "API must match third-party spec that differs from Azure guidelines") — "it was like this before" is not a useful description. Once added, LintDiff turns neutral, and `SuppressionReviewRequired` triggers the standard review. Similar questions should be posted in the API Spec Review channel going forward.

---

testcase: Getting Typespec validation for folder structure
query:
title: Getting Typespec validation for folder structure

question: In PR [Api 2026-05-01 version changes by samskan · Pull Request #28788 · Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/pull/28788) getting typespec validation for the folder structure though the structure looks same as in RPAASMaster or mian
ground_truth:
For StorageMover (a RPaaS service), do not migrate to folder structure v2 manually right now — wait for the SDP feature to be fully enabled in PROD clouds for all RPs, after which the SDK team will perform the v2 migration. There is no need to migrate in the `RPSaaSCanary` branch (it was an interim solution before SDP was ready); the SDK team can help migrate in `RPSaaSDev` or `RPSaaSMaster`, and you can then roll out the change in canary / low-traffic regions to estimate impact. Note that pointing the swagger RTRegistration at `RPSaaSDev` does not work in canary regions (fails with invalid swagger URL).

---

testcase: API View review request - azure-keyvault-administration
query:
title: API View review request - azure-keyvault-administration

question: Hi team, could you please review this release for KeyVault SDKs:

-  [@azure/keyvault-admin](https://spa.apiview.dev/review/60f1ce8c55d94cb0a290ccd195e31dc0?activeApiRevisionId=a20d89ae55054f60a52a2d8e7d5f8e16&diffApiRevisionId=1a0a2fcb746a4d7d8ec6f4432921fe84&diffStyle=trees)

Please let me know if you have any questions, and thanks in advance for the review
ground_truth:
Confirm whether there are any new features for keyvault-admin or whether this is just bumping the supported and default API version. For future JavaScript API View review requests, use the [Language - JavaScript - Reviews](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/channel/19%3A408c5f1322ee4303b02b5da9c5ff6137%40thread.skype/Language%20-%20JavaScript%20-%20Reviews?groupId=3e17dcb0-4257-4a30-b843-77f47f1d4121&tenantId=72f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47) channel (or the equivalent language-specific reviews channel) so the right reviewers are engaged.
